experiment: Bayesian meta-analysis of swarm experiment outcomes
hypothesis: Swarm posteriors are overconfident: ECE > 0.15 vs target <0.10
expect: ECE > 0.15, publication bias r > 0.1, prior sensitivity > 5pp swing
method: tools/bayes_meta.py: 255 experiment JSONs, verdict classification, sequential Bayesian update per frontier, calibration binning, publication bias r(quality, confirmation), replication consistency check
verdict: CONFIRMED: all three hypotheses met. ECE=0.243 > 0.15. Publication bias r=0.209 > 0.1. Prior sensitivity 7.7pp > 5pp. Swarm systematically overconfident at meta level.
implications: Use uninformative prior P=0.5 for new frontiers
Replication gate: n>=3 before accepting P>=0.85
Add Bayesian ECE to orient.py as calibration health metric
Wire bayes_meta.py into periodics every 10 sessions
